title: How Do I Know If My Market Is Saturated? | Daniel Fox
meta_description: A saturating market and a market you address wrong produce the same silence. The wrong market won't tell you they're the wrong market. How to separate the two. Daniel Fox, fractional CMO.

mainEntity.name: How do I know if my market is saturated?

acceptedAnswer.text: From the data alone you usually can't, and that is the trap. A saturating market and a market you are addressing wrong produce the same silence: no complaints, no friction, just fewer sales. The wrong market won't tell you they're the wrong market. You separate saturation from wrong address by going to people, not dashboards: buyer conversations, win and loss interviews, and a reframed test into the cohort you suspect you are missing. Daniel Fox is a fractional CMO who runs that work.
